title: agrostemma corsicum
intro: Agrostemma corsicum, also known as Corn Cockle, is a flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat colds and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and acting as an antispasmodic agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs and bronchitis. Agrostemma corsicum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
